title: Fields, Negotiation, Compression, Validators, and Caching · Contract Lens: Content Type Accept Negotiation
summary: The service naïvely splits repeated fields, sniffs content, omits Vary, compresses secrets, caches personalized responses, and computes unstable validators. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts content type accept negotiation increment in representation selection, compression, and cache contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate Content-Type parameters and select an acceptable representation with quality, specificity, fallback, and 406 or 415 behavior.
Handle case-insensitive names, repeated lines, list grammar, whitespace, forbidden hop-by-hop fields, header size, and Node joining behavior by field semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Content Type Accept Negotiation · Theory Content Type Accept Negoti…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8021 in a accessible status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep negotiation, validators, compression variance, and privacy decisions separate; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate Content-Type parameters and select an acceptable representation with quality, specificity, fallback, and 406 or 415 behavior.
